cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the text to figure out how long the boycott lasted. The text mentions that the boycott started in December 1955 when Rosa Parks was arrested. The boycott continued and was difficult for African Americans, but they kept it up. Then it says the U.S. Supreme Court ruled the laws unfair and gave equal rights. The boycott ended on December 21, 1956. So, from December 1955 to December 1956 is exactly one year. However, the Supreme Court decision that led to the end of the boycott happened after that year.
